bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with their worst possible offer for me (all F values) and claimed it's a take-it-or-leave-it deal. This is classic aggressive opening tactics and I should ignore the threatening message. At t=0.00, I have plenty of time to negotiate, so I'll counter with my optimal bid (all top values) to anchor the negotiation high. My best bid is issueA:valueA, issueB:valueA, issueC:valueA, issueD:valueA which gives me maximum utility.

The opponent is repeating the same lowball offer (all valueF) while making aggressive threats - classic cheap talk that contradicts any cooperative intent. We're only at t=0.02, very early in the negotiation, so there's no need to concede at all. The opponent's offer gives me very low utility (0.3*0.20 + 0.2*0.29 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.2*0.35 ≈ 0.225), while my top bid gives me maximum utility. I'll stick close to my ideal bid but perhaps make a tiny gesture on my lowest-weight issues to signal willingness to negotiate, while keeping issueA (weight 0.3) at my best value.

We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.04). The opponent is making aggressive threats but their behavior shows they are barely moving - they went from all valueF to one tiny change (issueD: valueF→valueE). Their messages are classic cheap talk ("I walk away permanently"). My max utility bid is all valueA. I should stay near my optimal bid since we have plenty of time. I'll keep my top bid since there's no reason to concede at this point.

The opponent is making aggressive threats but their behavior contradicts the "final offer" claims — they've already made a small concession (issueD: valueF → valueE). Their offers give me very low utility (all valueF/valueE = worst values). We're only at t=0.06, very early in the negotiation, so I should hold firm near my optimal bid. I'll maintain my top-utility offer since the opponent is clearly still negotiating despite their tough talk.

We're only at t=0.08, very early in the negotiation. The opponent is making threats ("take it or walk") but their actual behavior shows they ARE conceding slightly (issueD moved from valueF to valueE/valueC, issueC moved to valueC in latest offer). Their messages are classic aggressive cheap talk that contradicts their actual behavior of slowly moving. Their best offer to me so far gives very low utility (issueA:valueF=0.20*0.3 + issueB:valueF=0.29*0.2 + issueC:valueC=0.68*0.2 + issueD:valueF=0.35*0.2 ≈ 0.06+0.058+0.136+0.07 = ~0.32). I should continue holding relatively firm at this early stage, but maybe make a small concession to show willingness without giving much away. I'll keep my top values on the most important issues (issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA) but concede slightly on lower-weight issues.

The opponent keeps threatening to walk but continues making small concessions (they've moved on issueC and issueD slightly, and now issueA moved from valueF to valueE). Their messages are aggressive but their behavior shows they're still negotiating. We're only at t=0.10, so there's no need to rush concessions. My best offer (all valueA) gives utility 1.0. I should start making very minor concessions to signal some flexibility while staying high. I'll concede a tiny bit on issueD (my lowest-weight issue alongside B and C) by moving to valueB, which I already did last turn. Let me slightly adjust - keep issueA at valueA (highest weight), make a small concession on issueD to valueC to show movement, keep issueB and issueC at valueA.

The opponent keeps threatening to walk but continues making small concessions (issueD improved slightly, issueC improved, issueA moved from valueF to valueE). Their messages are cheap talk - they've made 7 offers despite claiming they'll "walk away." At t=0.12, we're still early in the negotiation. The opponent's best offer to me so far is still very low utility. I'll maintain a strong position but make a small concession on a lower-weight issue (issueD) to signal willingness to negotiate, while keeping my top values for issueA (weight 0.3). My utility: issueA=valueA(1.0*0.3) + issueB=valueA(1.0*0.2) + issueC=valueA(1.0*0.2) + issueD=valueC(0.48*0.2) = 0.3+0.2+0.2+0.096 = 0.796.
